search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae telehealth headache adults Issue 2 red flags emergency evaluation
candidate_sources_rejected: imaging, lumbar puncture, medicine and referral recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Undifferentiated headache may have primary or secondary causes.
No investigation, procedure, treatment, medicine, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with headache; children, pregnancy/postpartum, trauma and established neurological disease require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE emergency documentation.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence; UAE emergency, stroke, neurology, ophthalmology, infection, imaging and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag section supports onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and time-critical symptoms only when assessed; it does not infer a secondary cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact emergency-evaluation section supports recording measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance, existing results, escalation and specialist discussion only when clinician-completed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies headache presentations requiring urgent face-to-face assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The adult virtual-care scope was reviewed; pediatric, pregnancy, trauma and established neurological diagnoses require separate evidence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines adult headache telehealth scope in DHA-licensed facilities and emergency exclusions.

unresolved_source_gaps: Detailed onset and phenotype, prior history, pregnancy/postpartum, trauma, neurological and fundoscopic examination, tests, diagnosis, treatment, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
